note: Simplified reference summaries. The FBC Residential incorporates the IRC with Florida-specific amendments. Non-amended sections follow IRC 2021. For full code text, consult https://floridabuilding.org/

FL_R301.2.title: Florida Climatic and Geographic Design Criteria
FL_R301.2.summary: Florida-specific design criteria table. All of Florida is in seismic design category A or B. Wind speeds range from 115-195 mph ultimate. No frost depth or snow load. Extremely heavy termite hazard statewide.

subsections.FL_R301.2.1: Wind design criteria - Ultimate design wind speed per ASCE 7 Figure 1609.3. Risk Category II ranges from 115 mph (panhandle) to 195 mph (HVHZ Keys). Exposure C typical for coastal, D for Keys and barrier islands
subsections.FL_R301.2.2: Seismic design - SDC A or B for all Florida locations. No special seismic detailing required
subsections.FL_R301.2.3: Frost depth - 0 inches for all Florida locations (no frost line requirement)
subsections.FL_R301.2.4: Termite - Very heavy hazard for entire state. Subterranean and drywood termite protection required
subsections.FL_R301.2.5: Weathering - Negligible to moderate concrete weathering
subsections.FL_R301.2.6: Flood - FEMA flood maps and coastal construction control lines govern
subsections.FL_R301.2.7: Design wind speed examples - Orlando: 130 mph, Jacksonville: 130 mph, Tampa: 140 mph, Fort Lauderdale: 175 mph (HVHZ), Miami: 180 mph (HVHZ), Key West: 195 mph

FL_R322.title: Flood-Resistant Construction (Florida Amendments)
FL_R322.summary: Enhanced flood-resistant construction requirements for Florida's extensive coastal areas and flood zones.

subsections.FL_R322.1: General - Buildings in flood hazard areas shall be designed per ASCE 24 and local floodplain ordinance
subsections.FL_R322.2: Flood hazard areas (A Zones) - Lowest floor elevated to BFE +1 ft minimum (freeboard). Enclosures below BFE limited to parking, access, storage
subsections.FL_R322.3: Coastal high-hazard areas (V Zones) - Pile/column foundations, open foundation below BFE, bottom of lowest structural member at or above BFE
subsections.FL_R322.3.2: Coastal A zones - Treated as V zones when wave heights may exceed 1.5 ft. CCCL determines coastal construction standards
subsections.FL_R322.4: Manufactured homes - HUD standards plus local floodplain management
subsections.FL_R322.5: Breakaway walls - Below BFE in V zones, walls must be designed to break away under flood loads (max 20 psf)

FL_R609.title: Wall Bracing for Hurricane Regions
FL_R609.summary: Florida amendments to wall bracing requirements for high-wind regions.

subsections.FL_R609.1: General - Wall bracing per IRC Section R602.10 with Florida amendments for wind speeds exceeding 130 mph
subsections.FL_R609.2: Braced wall panels - Continuous structural panel sheathing (wood structural panels) required for wind speeds >130 mph
subsections.FL_R609.3: Connections - Hold-down anchors at braced wall panel ends for uplift resistance
subsections.FL_R609.4: HVHZ requirements - Engineered wall bracing required in Miami-Dade and Broward counties

FL_R610.title: Wall Anchorage for High Wind
FL_R610.summary: Florida-specific wall anchorage requirements for hurricane-prone regions.

subsections.FL_R610.1: General - Continuous load path from roof to foundation required
subsections.FL_R610.2: Anchor bolts - 1/2 in. anchor bolts at 4 ft OC max (more stringent than IRC 6 ft OC)
subsections.FL_R610.3: Strap connections - Approved metal connectors (hurricane straps/clips) at all roof-to-wall, wall-to-floor, and floor-to-foundation connections
subsections.FL_R610.4: HVHZ - All connections engineered and product-approved for HVHZ design wind speed

FL_R802.title: Enhanced Roof Framing for High Wind
FL_R802.summary: Florida amendments to roof framing for hurricane resistance.

subsections.FL_R802.1: Roof-to-wall connections - Hurricane clips/straps required at every rafter/truss-to-wall connection. Toe-nailing alone not permitted in HVHZ
subsections.FL_R802.2: Roof sheathing attachment - 8d ring-shank nails at 6 in. OC edges and 6 in. OC field for wind speeds ≥130 mph. Enhanced schedules for higher wind speeds
subsections.FL_R802.3: Secondary water barrier - Required in HVHZ. Self-adhering polymer-modified bitumen or approved equivalent over entire roof deck
subsections.FL_R802.4: Roof covering attachment - Enhanced fastening per manufacturer and TAS 101/102/107 in HVHZ
subsections.FL_R802.5: Gable end bracing - Required for all gable roofs in wind speeds ≥130 mph. Retrofit bracing required per FL Statute 553.844

FL_R803.title: Roof Sheathing for High Wind
FL_R803.summary: Florida-specific roof sheathing requirements for wind resistance.

subsections.FL_R803.1: Minimum sheathing thickness - 15/32 in. (or 7/16 in. OSB) min for wind speeds ≥130 mph
subsections.FL_R803.2: Sheathing attachment - Per Table R803.1 based on design wind speed. Ring-shank or deformed-shank nails required for ≥130 mph
subsections.FL_R803.3: Edge support - Panel edges shall be supported by blocking, H-clips, or tongue-and-groove edges

FL_POOL.title: Florida Swimming Pool Safety
FL_POOL.summary: Florida-specific pool barrier and safety requirements per Florida Statute 515.

subsections.FL_pool_1: Pool barriers - Residential pools must have at least one approved barrier (screen enclosure counts in FL)
subsections.FL_pool_2: Screen enclosures - May serve as pool barrier if self-closing, self-latching, minimum 48 in. high
subsections.FL_pool_3: Door alarms - Required on doors providing direct access from dwelling to pool area if no screen enclosure
subsections.FL_pool_4: Suction entrapment prevention - Compliant drain covers per Virginia Graeme Baker Act

FL_ROOFING.title: Florida Roofing Requirements
FL_ROOFING.summary: Florida-specific roofing material and installation requirements for high-wind regions.

subsections.FL_roof_1: Product approval - All roofing products must have Florida Product Approval or Miami-Dade NOA (in HVHZ)
subsections.FL_roof_2: Asphalt shingles - ASTM D3462, wind resistance per ASTM D7158 Class H (150 mph) for most FL locations. TAS 107 for HVHZ
subsections.FL_roof_3: Metal roofing - Must be tested per TAS 125/UL 580. Exposed fastener panels limited in HVHZ
subsections.FL_roof_4: Tile roofing - Mechanically attached per high-wind requirements. Mortar-set limited based on wind speed
subsections.FL_roof_5: Underlayment - Self-adhering modified bitumen or 2 layers of ASTM D226 Type II. Enhanced underlayment in HVHZ
subsections.FL_roof_6: Existing roofs - FL limits re-cover to 1 additional layer. Complete tear-off required when existing materials are wet, deteriorated, or 2 layers exist

_cross_reference.title: IRC 2021 Cross-Reference
_cross_reference.summary: For sections not specifically amended by Florida, refer to the IRC 2021 edition.

subsections.note_2: R303-R305 (Light, Ventilation, Room Areas, Ceiling Heights) - Follows IRC 2021
subsections.note_4: R313 (Fire Sprinklers) - Florida allows local jurisdiction to decide enforcement
subsections.note_5: R401-R404 (Foundations) - Follows IRC 2021 (no frost depth requirement in FL)
subsections.note_6: R502-R602 (Floor/Wall Framing) - Follows IRC 2021 with enhanced connection requirements
subsections.note_7: R703 (Exterior Covering) - Follows IRC 2021 with FL moisture/wind amendments
